title: One Night in the Deep Dark

pageTimings.text:
It was the deepest part of the night.
Moonwood was very quiet.
Then there was a sound at the edge of the clearing.
Something small was there.

Pip opened the door.
A small creature was at the edge of the clearing.
It had very large silver eyes.
It was frightened.
It did not run.

Pip knelt down.
He held still.
The creature came closer.
One step.
Then another.
"Hello," said Pip, very quietly.

Pip brought it inside.
Everyone woke up.
Stone brought a piece of acorn biscuit.
The creature ate it very carefully.
It watched everyone with its large silver eyes.

"Where is your home?" asked Burrow, gently.
The creature looked at its paws.
Then at the window.
"Deep," it said.
"Deep dark."

"We'll take you home," said Pip.
"But the Deep Dark—" said Wren.
"We'll go together," said Pip.
Everyone looked at each other.
"Together," said Stone.

They all went together.
Luna led — her eyes could see in any dark.
Glimmer breathed a small flame for the path.
The creature walked between them.
Into the deep.

The Deep Dark was very dark.
The trees had no glow.
The light went only a little way ahead.
They walked close together.
No one was last.
No one was alone.

Burrow found the underground path.
He could smell the way.
The creature stepped forward.
It knew this place.
"Here," it said.

Beyond the roots, a small clearing.
Silver moss lit the ground.
It was warm.
Other creatures were there.
They looked up.
They had the same enormous silver eyes.

The creature ran to its family.
The family gathered around it.
They looked at the Moonwood creatures.
Not afraid.
Just looking.
Then one of them blinked.

They walked back through the Dark.
At the edge, Pip looked back.
One silver light blinked twice.
"That was brave," said Glimmer.
Pip touched the stone in his pocket.
"Yes," he said. "It ran home."
"That's the bravest kind."
